description.content:
Postman collection for the InfraPortal CRM platform (v1.16.3 PR2), generated from the eleven per-service OpenAPI 3.0.3 specs by postman/generate-collection.mjs. Do not edit by hand; edit the specs and regenerate.

All runtime endpoints have been offline since 2026-06-04 (infrastructure decommissioned to zero); the collection documents the API contract as implemented in code. Point {{baseUrl}} at a locally running stack (default http://localhost:8080, the local go-gateway) and supply a dev JWT in {{token}}. See postman/README.md and docs/API.md.

CRM accounts service for the InfraPortal platform. Rust (axum 0.8) with
PostgreSQL persistence. Provides CRUD operations on customer accounts plus
health and readiness probes.

This spec documents the API contract as implemented in code
(src/lib/router.rs, src/lib/handlers/, src/lib/models.rs, src/lib/auth.rs).
All runtime endpoints have been offline since 2026-06-04, when the platform
infrastructure was decommissioned to zero. See ROADMAP.md in the repo root.

Authentication: every /api/v1 route validates a bearer JWT in the handler
and additionally requires the "admin" role. Requests without a valid token
receive 401; valid tokens without the admin role receive 403 with body
{"code": "FORBIDDEN", "message": "admin role required"}.

Error envelope: service-generated errors use the ApiError shape
{"code", "message", "details?"}. Framework-generated rejections (malformed
JSON, JSON body that fails deserialization, invalid query string types) are
produced by axum before the handler runs and have text/plain bodies; they
are documented per operation below.

Rate limiting: enforced at the platform gateway (go-gateway, shipped in
v1.10), not in this service. The gateway sets X-RateLimit-Limit,
X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset on every proxied response and
adds Retry-After on 429. Platform tiers: auth routes 5 rps, write routes
30 rps, read routes 60 rps, per client IP per route. Requests sent directly
to the service bypass the gateway and carry none of these headers.

then id descending. Supports filtering by status, case-insensitive

Restrict results to accounts owned by this user ID.

item.name: Accounts

description.content:
CRM activities service for the InfraPortal platform. Rust (axum 0.8) with
PostgreSQL persistence. Provides CRUD operations on activities (calls,
emails, meetings, tasks) linked optionally to accounts and contacts, plus
health and readiness probes.

This spec documents the API contract as implemented in code
(src/lib/router.rs, src/lib/handlers/, src/lib/models.rs, src/lib/auth.rs).
All runtime endpoints have been offline since 2026-06-04, when the platform
infrastructure was decommissioned to zero. See ROADMAP.md in the repo root.

Authentication: every /api/v1 route validates a bearer JWT in the handler
and additionally requires the "admin" role. Requests without a valid token
receive 401; valid tokens without the admin role receive 403 with body
{"code": "FORBIDDEN", "message": "admin role required"}. Because the admin
role is required on every route, the owner-scoped query paths that exist in
the handlers are unreachable: authenticated callers always operate on all
activities.

Error envelope: service-generated errors use the ApiError shape
{"code", "message", "details?"}. Handler validation failures in this
service use HTTP 422 with the ApiError envelope. Framework-generated
rejections (malformed JSON syntax, JSON body that fails deserialization)
are produced by axum before the handler runs and have text/plain bodies;
they are documented per operation below. No route in this service takes
query parameters, so query-string rejections cannot occur.

Rate limiting: enforced at the platform gateway (go-gateway, shipped in
v1.10), not in this service. The gateway sets X-RateLimit-Limit,
X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset on every proxied response and
adds Retry-After on 429. Platform tiers: auth routes 5 rps, write routes
30 rps, read routes 60 rps, per client IP per route. Requests sent directly
to the service bypass the gateway and carry none of these headers.

description.content:
Creates an activity owned by the caller (owner_id is taken from the
JWT sub claim). activity_type and subject are trimmed and must be
non-empty; violations return 422 with the ApiError envelope. notes and
due_at are stored verbatim (not trimmed) and default to null.
completed is always false at creation and cannot be set here.

If account_id is provided it is verified against accounts-service
(GET /api/v1/accounts/{id}, forwarding the caller's Authorization
header); likewise contact_id against contacts-service. A failed lookup
returns 422 with code INVALID_ACCOUNT or INVALID_CONTACT. When the
ACCOUNTS_SERVICE_URL or CONTACTS_SERVICE_URL environment variable is
unset the corresponding check is skipped entirely (fail-open); when it
is set but the HTTP call errors, the check fails closed and returns
422.

On success the service also emits an activity.created platform event,
indexes the activity in search-service (both fire-and-forget), and
emits an audit event. Write tier: 30 rps at the gateway.

item.name: Activities

description.content:
Immutable audit trail service for the InfraPortal platform. Rust (axum 0.8)
with PostgreSQL persistence. CRM services forward mutation events here;
admins query the resulting append-only log. Provides audit event ingestion
and listing plus health and readiness probes. There are no update or delete
routes: audit events are write-once.

This spec documents the API contract as implemented in code
(src/lib/router.rs, src/lib/handlers/, src/lib/models.rs, src/lib/auth.rs).
All runtime endpoints have been offline since 2026-06-04, when the platform
infrastructure was decommissioned to zero. See ROADMAP.md in the repo root.

Authentication: both /api/v1 routes validate a bearer JWT in the handler.
Requests without a valid token receive 401. Role requirements differ per
operation: GET /api/v1/audit-events requires the "admin" role (403 body
{"code": "FORBIDDEN", "message": "admin role required"});
POST /api/v1/audit-events accepts either the "admin" or the "service" role
(403 body {"code": "FORBIDDEN", "message": "admin or service role
required"}). CRM services forward the caller's admin token; the "service"
role covers machine writers. /health and /ready are unauthenticated.

Error envelope: service-generated errors use the ApiError shape
{"code", "message", "details?"}. Framework-generated rejections (malformed
JSON, JSON body that fails deserialization, invalid query string types) are
produced by axum before the handler runs and have text/plain bodies; they
are documented per operation below. Note that this service also generates
handler-level 422 responses in the ApiError envelope when entity_type or
action is not in the accepted set.

Rate limiting: enforced at the platform gateway (go-gateway, shipped in
v1.10), not in this service. The gateway sets X-RateLimit-Limit,
X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset on every proxied response and
adds Retry-After on 429. Platform tiers: auth routes 5 rps, write routes
30 rps, read routes 60 rps, per client IP per route. Requests sent directly
to the service bypass the gateway and carry none of these headers.


Contact Support:
 Name: InfraPortal platform

Audit event ingestion and query. Listing requires a bearer JWT with the
admin role; ingestion requires the admin or the service role.

description.content:
Returns a paginated list of audit events ordered by created_at
descending, then id descending. Supports exact-match filtering on
entity_type, entity_id, actor_id, and action, plus inclusive
created_at range bounds (created_after, created_before). Filter values
are compared exactly and case-sensitively against stored values;
entity_type and action are stored lowercased, so pass lowercase
values. Requires the admin role (the service role is NOT sufficient
for listing). Read tier: 60 rps at the gateway.

Exact match on entity_type. Stored values are lowercase; the
filter value is not validated against the accepted set, so an
unknown or wrongly cased value simply matches nothing.
Exact match on the audited entity's ID.
Exact match on the acting user's ID.

Exact match on action. Stored values are lowercase (created,
updated, deleted); the filter value is not validated, so an
unknown value simply matches nothing.

Inclusive lower bound on created_at (created_at >= value).
Timestamps are stored as TEXT in %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ format and
compared lexicographically, so the value must use the same format
to be meaningful.

Inclusive upper bound on created_at (created_at <= value). Same
TEXT lexicographic comparison caveat as created_after.

Maximum number of rows to return. Defaults to 50. Values are
clamped to the range 1 to 200 rather than rejected. Parsed as an
unsigned integer, so negative values are rejected with 400 by the
axum Query extractor.

Number of rows to skip. Defaults to 0. Parsed as an unsigned
integer, so negative values are rejected with 400 by the axum
Query extractor.

Records a single immutable audit event. Requires the admin or the
service role. entity_type, entity_id, action, and actor_id are
required, trimmed, and limited to 255 characters. entity_type and
action are matched case-insensitively against fixed sets (entity_type:
account, contact, opportunity, activity; action: created, updated,
deleted) and stored lowercased; a trimmed non-empty value outside the
set is rejected with a handler-generated 422 in the ApiError envelope.
entity_label is optional; a blank or whitespace-only value is stored
as null. payload accepts any JSON value; it is serialized to a compact
JSON string for storage and is returned as that string, not as the
original JSON structure. The service generates the event id (UUID v4)
and created_at (UTC, second precision); clients cannot supply them.
On success, when OBSERVABOARD_INGEST_URL and OBSERVABOARD_API_KEY are
configured, the event is also forwarded to Observaboard
(fire-and-forget; forwarding failures never affect the response).
Write tier: 30 rps at the gateway.

lowercased; payload is returned as a JSON-encoded string.

description.content:
CRM workflow automation service for the InfraPortal platform. Rust
(axum 0.8) with PostgreSQL persistence. Provides CRUD operations on
automation workflows (trigger event to action type mappings) plus health
and readiness probes.

This spec documents the API contract as implemented in code
(src/lib/router.rs, src/lib/handlers/, src/lib/models.rs, src/lib/auth.rs).
All runtime endpoints have been offline since 2026-06-04, when the platform
infrastructure was decommissioned to zero. See ROADMAP.md in the repo root.

Authentication: every /api/v1 route validates a bearer JWT in the handler
and additionally requires the "admin" role. Requests without a valid token
receive 401; valid tokens without the admin role receive 403 with body
{"code": "FORBIDDEN", "message": "admin role required"}. /health and
/ready are unauthenticated.

Error envelope: service-generated errors use the ApiError shape
{"code", "message", "details?"}. Handler validation failures in this
service return 422 with the ApiError envelope. Framework-generated
rejections (malformed JSON syntax, JSON body that fails deserialization)
are produced by axum before the handler runs and have text/plain bodies;
they are documented per operation below.

Rate limiting: enforced at the platform gateway (go-gateway, shipped in
v1.10), not in this service. The gateway sets X-RateLimit-Limit,
X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset on every proxied response and
adds Retry-After on 429. Platform tiers: auth routes 5 rps, write routes
30 rps, read routes 60 rps, per client IP per route. Requests sent directly
to the service bypass the gateway and carry none of these headers.

description.content:
CRM contacts service for the InfraPortal platform. Rust (axum 0.8) with
PostgreSQL persistence. Provides CRUD operations on contact records plus
health and readiness probes.

This spec documents the API contract as implemented in code
(src/lib/router.rs, src/lib/handlers/, src/lib/models.rs, src/lib/auth.rs).
All runtime endpoints have been offline since 2026-06-04, when the platform
infrastructure was decommissioned to zero. See ROADMAP.md in the repo root.

Authentication: every /api/v1 route validates a bearer JWT in the handler
and additionally requires the "admin" role (require_admin). Requests
without a valid token receive 401; valid tokens without the admin role
receive 403 with body
{"code": "FORBIDDEN", "message": "admin role required"}. The handlers
contain owner-scoped query branches for non-admin callers, but they are
unreachable on this branch because require_admin rejects non-admin tokens
before any query runs.

Error envelope: service-generated errors use the ApiError shape
{"code", "message", "details?"}. Framework-generated rejections (malformed
JSON, JSON body that fails deserialization, invalid query string types) are
produced by axum before the handler runs and have text/plain bodies; they
are documented per operation below.

Cross-service validation: when a request supplies a non-blank account_id,
the service verifies it with a GET to accounts-service
(ACCOUNTS_SERVICE_URL). A non-2xx or failed call yields 422 with
{"code": "INVALID_ACCOUNT", "message": "account not found"}. If
ACCOUNTS_SERVICE_URL is unset the check is skipped (fail-open for local
development).

Rate limiting: enforced at the platform gateway (go-gateway, shipped in
v1.10), not in this service. The gateway sets X-RateLimit-Limit,
X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset on every proxied response and
adds Retry-After on 429. Platform tiers: auth routes 5 rps, write routes
30 rps, read routes 60 rps, per client IP per route. Requests sent directly
to the service bypass the gateway and carry none of these headers.

description.content:
Returns a paginated list of contacts ordered by last_name ascending,
then first_name ascending. Supports filtering by account_id and
lifecycle_stage (exact match), case-insensitive substring search (q)

Maximum number of rows to return. Defaults to 50. Values are

non-numeric values are rejected with 400 by the query extractor.
Number of rows to skip. Defaults to 0.
Restrict results to contacts linked to this account ID (exact match).

Filter by exact lifecycle stage. The handler does not validate
this value against the accepted set; an unknown value is not an
error, it simply matches no rows.

Case-insensitive substring match (SQL ILIKE) applied to
first_name, last_name, and email; a row matches if any of the
three fields matches.
Restrict results to contacts owned by this user ID.

Creates a contact owned by the caller (owner_id is taken from the JWT
sub claim). first_name and last_name are trimmed and must be non-empty
and at most 255 characters. lifecycle_stage is trimmed and defaults to
"lead" when omitted; an unknown value is rejected with 400. A blank or
whitespace-only account_id, email, or phone is stored as null. A
non-blank account_id is verified against accounts-service; an unknown
account yields 422 INVALID_ACCOUNT (the check is skipped when
ACCOUNTS_SERVICE_URL is unset). email is limited to 255 characters
after trimming; this check runs after account validation, so an
invalid account_id wins over an overlong email. phone has no length
limit. On success the service also emits a contact.created platform
event, indexes the contact in search-service, and emits an audit event
(all fire-and-forget). Write tier: 30 rps at the gateway.

description.content:
Third-party integration connections service for the InfraPortal platform.
Rust (axum 0.8) with PostgreSQL persistence. Provides CRUD operations on
integration connection records (provider, account reference, sync status)
plus health and readiness probes.

This spec documents the API contract as implemented in code
(src/lib/router.rs, src/lib/handlers/, src/lib/models.rs, src/lib/auth.rs).
All runtime endpoints have been offline since 2026-06-04, when the platform
infrastructure was decommissioned to zero. See ROADMAP.md in the repo root.

Authentication: every /api/v1 route validates a bearer JWT in the handler
and additionally requires the "admin" role. Requests without a valid token
receive 401; valid tokens without the admin role receive 403 with body
{"code": "FORBIDDEN", "message": "admin role required"}. /health and /ready
are unauthenticated.

with PostgreSQL persistence. Provides CRUD operations on sales
opportunities plus health and readiness probes.

This spec documents the API contract as implemented in code
(src/lib/router.rs, src/lib/handlers/, src/lib/models.rs, src/lib/auth.rs).
All runtime endpoints have been offline since 2026-06-04, when the platform

and additionally requires the "admin" role. /health and /ready are open.

JSON syntax, JSON body that fails deserialization, undecodable query
strings) are produced by axum before the handler runs and have text/plain
bodies; they are documented per operation below. Because the body
extractor runs before the in-handler auth check, a malformed body is
rejected with 400 or 422 even when the request carries no token.

Rate limiting: enforced at the platform gateway (go-gateway, shipped in
v1.10), not in this service. The gateway sets X-RateLimit-Limit,
X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset on every proxied response and
adds Retry-After on 429. Platform tiers: auth routes 5 rps, write routes
30 rps, read routes 60 rps, per client IP per route. Requests sent directly
to the service bypass the gateway and carry none of these headers.

item.name: Opportunities

description.content:
Client portal projects service for the InfraPortal platform. Rust
(axum 0.8) with PostgreSQL persistence. Manages projects, milestones,
deliverables, portal messages, external links, and synced email threads,
plus health and readiness probes.

This spec documents the API contract as implemented in code
(src/lib/router.rs, src/lib/handlers/, src/lib/models.rs, src/lib/auth.rs).
All runtime endpoints have been offline since 2026-06-04, when the platform
infrastructure was decommissioned to zero. See ROADMAP.md in the repo root.

Authentication: mixed model, validated in-handler on every /api/v1 route.
Requests without a valid bearer JWT receive 401. Role behavior:

- admin: full access to every route.
- client: read access (list/get projects, milestones, deliverables,
  messages, links, emails) scoped to projects whose client_user_id equals
  the JWT sub claim, plus the ability to post messages on those projects.
  A client probing a project (or milestone under a project) that is not
  assigned to them receives 404 with the same body as a missing row, not
  403. This is by design: cross-client probes are indistinguishable from
  nonexistent resources, so project existence is never disclosed.
- any other role: 403 {"code": "FORBIDDEN", "message": "admin or client
  role required"} on the read routes and on message creation.

Every mutation except message creation (create/update/delete of projects,
milestones, deliverables, links, and the email sync) is admin-only and
returns 403 {"code": "FORBIDDEN", "message": "admin role required"} for
any non-admin caller, including clients. /health and /ready are
unauthenticated.

Error envelope: service-generated errors use the ApiError shape
{"code", "message", "details?"}. Framework-generated rejections (malformed
JSON, JSON body that fails deserialization) are produced by axum before
the handler runs and have text/plain bodies; they are documented per
operation below.

Rate limiting: enforced at the platform gateway (go-gateway, shipped in
v1.10), not in this service. The gateway sets X-RateLimit-Limit,
X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset on every proxied response and
adds Retry-After on 429. Platform tiers: auth routes 5 rps, write routes
30 rps, read routes 60 rps, per client IP per route. Requests sent directly
to the service bypass the gateway and carry none of these headers.

item.name: Sync email threads onto a project

description.content:
Upserts a batch of email threads onto the project, keyed on
(project_id, thread_id): existing threads have subject, from_email,
snippet, body_html, received_at, and updated_at replaced; new threads
are inserted. Admin-only. The project existence check runs before any
email validation, so an unknown project_id returns 404 even when the
body is also invalid. Emails are validated and upserted one at a time
in request order with no transaction: when validation fails partway
through the list, the emails before the failing one are already
upserted and the 400 response does not roll them back. An empty
emails array succeeds with {"upserted": 0}. received_at is stored as
sent (no date validation). Write tier: 30 rps at the gateway.

item.name: Emails

description.content:
CRM reporting service for the InfraPortal platform. Rust (axum 0.8) with
PostgreSQL persistence. Provides CRUD on saved report definitions, CSV and
JSON export, and dashboard aggregates that combine local report counts
with totals fetched live from the accounts, contacts, opportunities, and
activities services, plus health and readiness probes.

This spec documents the API contract as implemented in code
(src/lib/router.rs, src/lib/handlers/, src/lib/models.rs, src/lib/auth.rs).
All runtime endpoints have been offline since 2026-06-04, when the platform
infrastructure was decommissioned to zero. See ROADMAP.md in the repo root.

Authentication: every /api/v1 route, including /api/v1/dashboard, validates
a bearer JWT in the handler and additionally requires the "admin" role.
/health and /ready are unauthenticated. Requests without a valid token
receive 401; valid tokens without the admin role receive 403 with body
{"code": "FORBIDDEN", "message": "admin role required"}. Because the admin
role is required on every route, the owner-scoped (non-admin) query
branches that exist in the handlers are unreachable on this branch: every
authorized caller sees all reports.

Error envelope: service-generated errors use the ApiError shape
{"code", "message", "details?"}. Framework-generated rejections (malformed
JSON, JSON body that fails deserialization, duplicated query parameters)
are produced by axum before the handler runs and have text/plain bodies;
they are documented per operation below. Body-validation failures in the
create and update handlers return 422 with the ApiError envelope and code
VALIDATION_ERROR; the export format check is the one handler-level 400,
with code INVALID_FORMAT.

Rate limiting: enforced at the platform gateway (go-gateway, shipped in
v1.10), not in this service. The gateway sets X-RateLimit-Limit,
X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset on every proxied response and
adds Retry-After on 429. Platform tiers: auth routes 5 rps, write routes
30 rps, read routes 60 rps, per client IP per route. Requests sent directly
to the service bypass the gateway and carry none of these headers.


Contact Support:
 Name: InfraPortal platform

description.content:
Aggregates the local reports count and distinct metric list with entity
totals fetched from sibling services (accounts, contacts,
opportunities, activities). Each sibling total is null when the
corresponding *_SERVICE_URL environment variable is unset or empty,
when the downstream call returns a non-2xx status, or when the
downstream body carries no numeric "total" field. The caller's Authorization header is
forwarded verbatim to the sibling services.

When user_id is given, the reports count and the owner_id filter sent
to sibling services are scoped to that user; core_metrics is never
scoped. Query parameters are read as free-form strings, so there is no
typed query rejection on this route; unknown parameters are ignored.

stage_distribution and recent_activities are reserved fields that the
handler hardcodes to null on this branch. Requires the admin role.
Read tier: 60 rps at the gateway.

item.name: Export reports as CSV or JSON

description.content:
Exports saved reports as a downloadable attachment, ordered by
created_at descending. format selects csv or json (default json,
matched case-insensitively; any other value returns 400 with code
INVALID_FORMAT). Optional filters: metric (exact, case-sensitive
equality), created_after and created_before (plain string comparison
against the stored created_at text, so pass full timestamps in
%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ format; the values are not parsed or validated).
All query parameters are read as optional strings, so wrong-type
query rejections cannot occur; the only Query extractor rejection is
a duplicated parameter (for example format given twice), which
returns 400 with a text/plain body. Requires the admin role, so all
reports are exported. Read tier: 60 rps at the gateway.

request.name: Export reports as CSV or JSON

description.content: Export format. Case-insensitive; defaults to json when omitted.
Only include reports whose metric equals this value exactly.

Only include reports whose created_at is greater than or equal to
this value (string comparison against the stored text).

Only include reports whose created_at is less than or equal to
this value (string comparison against the stored text).

item.name: Reports

description.content:
Platform search index for the InfraPortal CRM. Rust (axum 0.8) with
PostgreSQL persistence and an optional read replica: when
DATABASE_REPLICA_URL is set, search, list, and get queries run on the
replica pool while indexing writes and the health ping run on the primary.
Peer services (accounts-service and the other CRM services) push documents
into this index on entity create, update, and delete, fire-and-forget.

This spec documents the API contract as implemented in code
(src/lib/router.rs, src/lib/handlers/, src/lib/models.rs, src/lib/auth.rs).
All runtime endpoints have been offline since 2026-06-04, when the platform
infrastructure was decommissioned to zero. See ROADMAP.md in the repo root.

Authentication: every /api/v1 route validates a bearer JWT in the handler.
As of 2026-07-18 this service is authenticated but NOT role-gated on main:
the decoded roles claim is never checked, so any valid token can read and
write the search index, and this service never returns 403. The planned
gating (admin or service role required) is not yet implemented.

Error envelope: service-generated errors use the ApiError shape
{"code", "message", "details?"}; this service never populates details.
Framework-generated rejections (malformed JSON, JSON body that fails
deserialization, missing or invalid query parameters) are produced by axum
before the handler runs, which means before the auth check, and have
text/plain bodies; they are documented per operation below.

Rate limiting: enforced at the platform gateway (go-gateway, shipped in
v1.10), not in this service. The gateway sets X-RateLimit-Limit,
X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset on every proxied response and
adds Retry-After on 429. Platform tiers: auth routes 5 rps, write routes
30 rps, read routes 60 rps, per client IP per route. Requests sent directly
to the service bypass the gateway and carry none of these headers.


Contact Support:
 Name: InfraPortal platform

description.content:
Case-insensitive substring search (SQL LIKE) of the query term against
document title and body, returning lightweight results with truncated
snippets, ordered by created_at descending. The term is trimmed and
lowercased server-side before matching; an empty or whitespace-only
term short-circuits to 200 with an empty array without touching the
database. LIKE wildcards (percent and underscore) in the term are not
escaped and act as wildcards. Results are not paginated: every
matching row is returned. Runs on the read (replica) pool.
Read tier: 60 rps at the gateway.

(Required) Search term. Required: omitting the parameter entirely is rejected
with 400 by the axum Query extractor. Present-but-empty (q=) or
whitespace-only values return 200 with an empty array.

description.content:
Cloud spend tracking service for the InfraPortal platform. Rust (axum 0.8)
with PostgreSQL persistence. Provides CRUD operations on spend records,
aggregated spend summaries, billing sync pulls from GCP, Fly.io, GitHub,
and AWS, plus health and readiness probes.

This spec documents the API contract as implemented in code
(src/lib/router.rs, src/lib/handlers/, src/lib/models.rs, src/lib/auth.rs).
All runtime endpoints have been offline since 2026-06-04, when the platform
infrastructure was decommissioned to zero. See ROADMAP.md in the repo root.

Authentication: every /api/v1 route validates a bearer JWT in the handler.
Requests without a valid token receive 401. As implemented on main as of
2026-07-18 there is NO role gating: any valid JWT can call every route,
and the roles claim is parsed but never enforced. The only 403 responses
in this service are the automated-record guards on PATCH and DELETE
(records whose source is not "manual" cannot be edited or deleted).
Note: admin role gating is planned and arrives with the
refactor/adopt-axum-api-kit branch.

Error envelope: service-generated errors use the ApiError shape
{"code", "message", "details?"}. Framework-generated rejections (malformed
JSON, JSON body that fails deserialization, invalid query string types) are
produced by axum before the handler runs and have text/plain bodies; they
are documented per operation below.

Rate limiting: enforced at the platform gateway (go-gateway, shipped in
v1.10), not in this service. The gateway sets X-RateLimit-Limit,
X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset on every proxied response and
adds Retry-After on 429. Platform tiers: auth routes 5 rps, write routes
30 rps, read routes 60 rps, per client IP per route. Requests sent directly
to the service bypass the gateway and carry none of these headers.

and must be one of the known platforms. The date must be YYYY-MM-DD.
amount_usd must be non-negative. granularity is trimmed and defaults
to "daily" when omitted. service_label (max 255 characters) and notes
(max 1000 characters) are trimmed; blank or whitespace-only values are
stored as null. source is always "manual" for records created through
this endpoint. The record ID is a generated UUID v4 and timestamps are
set to the current UTC time. Write tier: 30 rps at the gateway.

item.description: Billing sync pulls from external platforms. Requires a bearer JWT (no role gating on main as of 2026-07-18).

description.content:
Pulls the last 30 days of GCP spend from the BigQuery billing export
(grouped by service and day) and inserts daily records with platform
"gcp" and source "bigquery". Duplicate rows are skipped via ON
CONFLICT DO NOTHING and counted in records_skipped. Requires the
GCP_BILLING_SA_KEY, GCP_BILLING_PROJECT_ID, GCP_BILLING_DATASET, and
GCP_BILLING_TABLE environment variables on the server. No request
body. Configuration, upstream, and per-row insert failures do NOT
produce error status codes: the handler always returns 200 with any
failures listed in the errors array. Write tier: 30 rps at the
gateway.
